headline: Coral Bleaching Hits 67% of Tioman Marine Park
context: The Department of Marine Parks confirmed in September 2024 that 67% of surveyed coral in Tioman Marine Park showed moderate to severe bleaching following record sea temperatures.

cards.big: Tourism Malaysia promotes Tioman as a 'pristine coral paradise.'
cards.sub: Marine Parks data shows 67% bleaching — the worst recorded since monitoring began in 2005.

cards.big: Sea surface temperatures hit 31.4°C in August 2024.
cards.sub: Coral bleaching threshold for Malaysian reefs is 30.5°C. Temperatures exceeded it for 47 consecutive days.

cards.big: Marine park entry fees have not increased since 2003.
cards.sub: RM5 per visitor generates RM2.1M annually — insufficient for reef restoration costing RM18M per year.

cards.big: 153 dive operators depend on Tioman reefs for livelihood.
cards.sub: No transition plan exists if reefs degrade beyond tourism viability within 10-15 years.

cards.big: Are we marketing a reef system to death while underfunding its survival?
Marine park fees should reflect ecological costs, not 2003 price points. RM5 entry fees for a dying ecosystem is a policy choice, not a fact of nature.
